mnemonics.title: GREP Meaning
mnemonics.content: GREP = Global Regular Expression Print - Think 'G'lobal means search everywhere, 'RE'gular 'P'rint what matches.

mnemonics.title: Anchors Remember
mnemonics.content: ^ is at the beginning (caret looks like start), $ is at the end (dollar sign = money at end).

mnemonics.title: Regex Quantifiers
mnemonics.content: * = Star = Start with zero (zero or more), + = Plus = Positive (one or more), ? = Question = Maybe (zero or one).

mnemonics.title: Pipe Direction
mnemonics.content: Think of | as a water pipe - water flows from left to right through it.

acronyms.usage: grep with Extended Regular Expressions support
grep that searches for fixed strings instead of patterns
Original regex syntax used by grep
Enhanced regex syntax used by egrep with additional metacharacters

visualTips.visualization: Imagine water flowing through a series of connected pipes - each command processes the water and passes it to the next.
Think of ^ as a hook at the start catching the line, and $ as a anchor holding the end of the line.
Think of [] as a team selection - choose which characters can 'play' (match).
The dot is a 'wildcard' like a blank tile in Scrabble - it can represent any single letter.

analogies.analogy: Like a factory assembly line: raw material (data) enters, each station (command) processes it, and final product emerges.
Like a blueprint or template - it describes a pattern rather than exact text, like a cookie cutter shapes many cookies.
Like using Ctrl+F in a document, but much more powerful - it's like having a smart search that understands patterns.
Think of metacharacters as 'wildcards' in a card game - they can represent multiple possible values.
